query: debug async race conditions in javascript stale ui cancellation request guards

competitors.label: AbortController
competitors.selectionReason: MDN is the canonical reference for request cancellation primitives, which are central to fixing stale async races.
competitors.winReason: MDN explains the API itself, but our page is more directly about the production bug pattern: latest-request races in search, autosave, and route changes, plus the distinction between true cancellation, non-abortable work, and stale-result guards.
theirStrengths: It is the authoritative reference for what AbortController is and how abortable requests work in the platform.
theirGaps: It is API-centric and less explicit about stale UI overwrites, request ordering bugs, and non-cancellation fallbacks.

realWorldUseCases: This is a classic production debug problem in search, filters, autosave flows, and route-driven detail pages.
followUpConfusion: You need either real cancellation or a stale-result guard.

realWorldUseCases: Our page is more concrete about the actual failure mode where request A overwrites newer request B in the UI.
actionableExamples: Our page now shows three practical patterns together: request-id guards, AbortController cancellation, and a non-abortable autosave guard, but MDN remains stronger on the raw platform API details.
followUpConfusion: Our answer is more explicit that cancellation is not the only fix and that stale-result guards are still needed in some flows.

actionableExamples: Add one compact shared-controller code snippet so the page covers grouped aborts as concretely as the platform docs.

theirStrengths: It directly covers latest-request prioritization and shows AbortController usage in interview-oriented code.
theirGaps: It is narrower on stale-result guards when true cancellation is unavailable or incomplete.

realWorldUseCases: This is a classic production debug problem in search, filters, autosave flows, and route-driven detail pages.
